stories.title: Iran Confirmed to Participate Despite Political Tensions
stories.desc: FIFA President Gianni Infantino confirmed at Vancouver Congress that Iran will compete in World Cup 2026 and play all matches in the US, with Trump backing the decision despite ongoing geopolitical tensions.

stories.title: Panini Launches Record-Breaking Sticker Collection
stories.desc: Panini's 2026 World Cup sticker album features 980 stickers, the largest edition ever, launched at Wembley Stadium with former England stars. The collection went on sale April 30 with starter packs available at retailers.

stories.title: Coca-Cola Partners with Panini for Special Stickers
stories.desc: Coca-Cola and Panini launched a first-of-its-kind partnership featuring 12 exclusive player stickers hidden behind labels on 20-oz bottles. The special stickers appear mid-May and include stars like Harry Kane and Lamine Yamal.

stories.title: FIFA Congress in Vancouver Addresses World Cup Preparations
stories.desc: The 76th FIFA Congress convened in Vancouver on April 30, focusing on the upcoming World Cup while dealing with geopolitical tensions. Prize pool increased to record $871 million and Afghanistan women's team cleared to compete.

stories.title: World Cup Tourism Drives Hotel Price Surge
stories.desc: Hotel prices across US, Canada, and Mexico host cities are rising dramatically as World Cup 2026 approaches. The tournament is reshaping tourism demand with connectivity gaps and capacity challenges emerging.

stories.title: Extreme Heat Could Threaten Players and Fans
stories.desc: Researchers warn that extreme heat poses significant risks to players, fans, and officials during World Cup 2026 matches starting in June. Rising global temperatures create new safety concerns for the tournament.
